text:
Last summer, in a quiet little town where the leaves never fell entirely and the days lingered long into twilight, I befriended a robin named Rufus. Rufus didn't wear much feather stuff except maybe a small plume on his head during spring migration. Every morning he would sing a bright little tune that seemed to dance around the garden.

You see, Rufus wasn't just any robin roaming the edges of my vegetable garden. He'd visit me countless times each day, hopping around as if eagerly anticipating a chat. One afternoon, as I sat reading under a big old weeping willow, he perched on a sturdy branch just a few feet away.

"I've been pondering," Rufus began, his voice a mixture of curiosity and mischief, "about winter. You humans love your cozy beds and warm beverages. What do you do in the winter?"

I chuckled. "Oh, just snuggle into blankets, brew some hot cocoa, read, dream about birds flying
